# Generated by ollama (qwen2.5-coder_14b)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Application, Router } from "https://deno.land/x/oak/mod.ts";

const app = new Application();
const router = new Router();

router.get("/load", async (ctx) => {
  const modulePath = ctx.request.url.searchParams.get("module") || Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");
  
  if (!modulePath) {
    ctx.response.status = 400;
    ctx.response.body = { error: "Module path is required" };
    return;
  }

  try {
    // Dynamically import the module
    const module = await import(modulePath);
    
    // Assuming the module has an export named 'execute'
    if (typeof module.execute === "function") {
      ctx.response.body = await module.execute();
    } else {
      ctx.response.status = 500;
      ctx.response.body = { error: "Module does not have an execute function" };
    }
  } catch (error) {
    ctx.response.status = 500;
    ctx.response.body = { error: `Failed to load module: ${error.message}` };
  }
});

app.use(router.routes());
app.use(router.allowedMethods());

await app.listen({ port: Deno.env.get("PORT") ? parseInt(Deno.env.get("PORT")) : 8000 });
